text:
Bug 1 - model loading state + which folder to use
Which folder: Use chatgpt_paraphraser_on_T5_base/ (complete: config.json, tokenizer.json, spiece.model, weights, etc.). Your model_files/ was incomplete - only weights + spiece.model, missing config.json and the tokenizer files, so from_pretrained() couldn't load it. No re-download needed - I copied the 5 small missing files into model_files/, so both folders now work.

Point Config -> Paraphrase model at:
D:\Workspace\AI,Automation\Human_Typing_Simulation\chatgpt_paraphraser_on_T5_base

Loading state feedback: added a Test button next to the model path in the config dialog. It loads the model on a background thread and pops up success (with a sample paraphrase) or failure (with the actual error). Paraphraser.test_load() now surfaces errors instead of swallowing them; normal typing still degrades gracefully if the model can't load. (Verified: here it correctly reports ModuleNotFoundError since transformers isn't installed in this sandbox - on your machine it's in requirements.txt, so it'll load and show a sample.)

Bug 2 - coding indentation uses Tab, not typed spaces
The screenshot's cascading indentation was our leading spaces compounding with the IDE's auto-indent. Now, in the coding rhythm, leading-line whitespace is emitted as Tab key presses (one per indent level), never individual spaces:

Auto-detects the indent unit (4 in your example).
->1 Tab, ->2 Tabs, ->3 Tabs. Verified: no space-runs sent, correct Tab counts, 200/200 convergence.
Since your IDE also auto-indents, one thing to watch: pressing Tab and the editor's auto-indent could still stack. If you still see over-indentation, the cleaner option for auto-indenting editors is to send nothing for indentation (let the IDE do it entirely) - tell me and I'll switch it, or make it a config toggle (Tab / none).

All on the err-modeling branch; flake8 clean, App + config dialog build, full engine convergence intact.
